So far so good! My first load was intentionally small to see how well it'd do and it kicked butt! Last weekend I did a week's worth of socks, drawers and tees since I have uniforms provided at work and it still kicked butt! What a great alternative to using the nasty pay washer and dryer at the end of the building. Does it leave tees wrinkled? Oh for sure, but if I want any of then wrinkle-free I can spray them with wrinkle release and smooth by hand OR iron. This is a nicely designed powered cooler. It is VERY quiet. You can hardly hear it. I like the feature that it can run off of 110 house current or your car battery. I was impressed how cold it kept items. I put a room temperature drink inside and within 90 minutes it was nice and cold. I have not used the warming feature but the instructions state it is not intended to heat food, just to keep it warm. It can heat food but it would take at least a couple of hours.

In summary, if you are in the need of a portable small powered cooler, this unit is an exceptional value.

Wow was I surprised at how heavy duty this product is! The MEANHDAG Outdoor Dryer Vent Closure is made of seriously heavy stainless steel, not thin sheet metal. The edges are smooth and not sharp at all. The finish is slick and smooth. The ad says it is paintable, but it looks too good to be painted. Very simple installation in most cases. Just slide it together and mount to wall with 6 included screws. They even include a long Philipps screwdriver and gloves. Your dryer pipe must stick out of the wall 1/2 inch to insert into this unit.

The design is simple and, if I dare say, elegant. The design keeps cold or hot backdraft air, insects, rodents, etc. from entering the house. I have not seen another product that does this so well.

Before receiving the product, what concerned me most was how the damper would work. But after seeing it, I think it is a great design. It has a light-weight floating shuttle cup damper in the top that the dryer air lifts to let the air escape. This works smoothly and should hold up for years. The only maintenance is to remove the stainless-steel cap occasionally to clean out any lint. And as you know, you have to do this with any other vent cover. I have vinyl siding and one of those vinyl vents with the little louvers that always clog with lint.

This is a versatile product that could be used on any fan air exhaust. The instructions say for use on smaller than 6 inch vents, but the opening of the unit is 4 inches. I know the price is kinda shocking, but what a great upgrade this product is! When you see the heavy gage steel and finish you will understand the price. It’s truly commercial/industrial grade. It has a one year warranty, but I doubt you will ever have to replace this thing. It’s not for everyone, but if you want a super high-quality vent cover, I don’t think you can beat this one.

DIY'ers can do this maintenance/repair easily. Keeping track of the screws and which go where is probably the most difficult part of the job. I luckily ordered the full kit since it wasn't much more and I felt I might as well replace the rollers if I'm going to be in there. Turns out the belt wasn't broken but came off because the tension pulley semi-froze from heat/lack of lubrication and kicked it off. That said the belt had a few knicks and needed to be replaced. Overall its a decent kit and my drying is running, but it's only been 2 days since install. Unfortunately both the OEM and this kit do not use bearings in the rollers or pulley, simply rotates/slide on their spindles.

Note the new roller wheel's inside diameter is just off of the OEM's and I do not suggest simply swapping the wheels onto the existing spindles. I only replaced the outer/front mounted wheels since i couldn't remove the nuts from the back wall spindles...and I really didn't care to show it who was boss at 10:30pm, plus the wheels did not look very worn. I was surprised they do not use any lubrication, that i could tell, on any of the wheels or pulley.

The tensioner pulley bracket is slightly different design from the OEM and probably the piece I'd be most concerned about failing based on slightly loose fit. Again the lack of bearings and/or lubrication seems like a set up to fail over time. Other's mentioned using the OEM bolt, but my bracket was such that it would not properly attach the new pully arm. All good so far. $35 spent seemingly well.

Part is a perfect match for my GE front load washer, model GFWN1000L1WW.  The issue I had was the water would not turn off during a cycle. I used the washer this way for longer than I want to admit and it makes the chore of doing laundry even worse.  This solved the problem and was really simple to replace.  I am a girl and I did it in less than 20 minutes with just a screwdriver and pliers.  Watched a couple YouTube videos and was surprised how easy it was.  Seriously, it is two screws and switching 3 hoses and the electrical parts.  Bam! Done! Washer is running perfect now.  Can only imagine the money I saved doing it myself.

This washer is defectively engineered. Whirlpool wanted to create a top loading, water sparing machine, so their idea was to come up with a tiny agitator on the bottom of the tank and allow it to "wobble" to force the water through the clothing. The water used for washing does not cover the clothing, and the agitator kind of tries to move the water through the clothing.

That does NOT happen. The clothes do NOT come out clean. I suppose it is possible to wash similar size, small items like underwear in it. But you can't mix clothing or bedding types in the same load.

Compare this pathetic machine with a modern front load washer that uses gravity to tumble the clothes against each other in two directions, as well as allowing them to soak momentarily on the bottom of the tub with each change of direction. You can't help but wash the clothes.

Cabrio runs the clothing through a very thorough rinse cycle, trying to make up for the deficiencies of washing. The clothes get all tangled and knotted up. It also unbalances easily as a washed sheet will eventually gather up smaller items into a ball and the agitator will just smash against that ball of sheet and underwear.

I submitted my review to Whirlpool and I suggested they redesign the washer and tilt the clothing tub to a 35% angle, which would start to use gravity...but that would expand the footprint of the machine. They said no-way and took my review off line.

I sent mine back after 4 days and paid the restocking fee. It is the worst washer made. You'd be better off using your great-grandmother's washer with an attached hand wringer. (Watch your fingers). There have also been technical, electrical and mechanical problems with it, even after a short term of ownership.

I got a Maytag front loader instead, and it works great.

I think the Cabrio is best used as land-fill.

The unit itself is whisper quiet when running and has an appealing look to it with the controls being touch screen and light up when the upper door panel is touched. The reversible door seems easy to change given the instructions and hinges supplied. The door operation is smooth and returns well to a closed position on its own. With the interior light that responds quickly to a door open and close position this is the ideal set up.

I certainly have no complaints about this product and would highly recommend its purchase whether for massive wine storage, beverage cooling, or ultimately, as I intend to be used as a cheese aging cave.

The quality of the product was good, but it did not appear to be the direct replacement for the original pulley.  It was larger OD by approximately 1/4".  The original slid off the shaft easily, but the replacement was a heavy interference fit.  I had to lubricate the shaft and tap the new pulley into place where it stopped 1/2 way on.  When attempting remove the stuck replacement pulley off the shaft, the plastic pulley separated from its metal hub.  I used channel locks against the end of the hub and the backside of the bracket to move the hub into proper position.  Once in position I installed the plastic pulley onto the hub.  It turned very tight, but once I put the belt back in place and started the drier it ran fine and the squealing was gone. My rating would have been higher but for the difficulty in installation.  It turned a 10 minute job into 70 minutes.

Overall this is a useful product. We don't raise chickens, but my family does go through eggs like crazy! I made labels for the drawers with my Cricut, and now I love it that much more! So here are my thoughts:

PROS
* I love having all the eggs together, while still being able to keep the raw and hardboiled eggs separate.
* Easy to clean.
* Eggs don't bump or wobble into each other.
* Compact size easily fits most shelves in my fridge.

CONS
* I wish it had feet to prevent it from sliding around the fridge shelf. I tried stick-on rubber dots but they didn't help at all. A paper towel under the unit helped more, but ultimately I ended up using some of that blue tacky stuff on the bottom corners (see 3rd photo) and so far that's been the most effective.
* There's no stopper on the drawers to prevent them from quickly sliding all the way out if you're not careful. 2 dozen raw eggs on the floor taught me this lesson. Oye! Haven't Maguyver'ed a solution for this yet. Still working on it...

If they found a way to fix those 2 little issues then it would be perfect. Still though, I'm glad I finally bought it!

This new Snoworld Countertop Ice Maker is a very good machine. It has some unique features that makes it stand out from competing Ice Makers in the same price class. It makes plenty of bullet-type ice cubes in two sizes and does it as quickly as similar ice makers. It does have a couple of minor annoyances, but they are not deal-breakers, and overall, the unit is a good potential choice for a number of uses as described below.

PROS:
* Well-made and very robust construction. It's a tank of a machine.
* Produces first ice within 8-minutes of first start.
* Two cube sizes to choose from, though the vast majority of users are likely to choose Large.
* Self-Cleaning cycle which is the first I've seen on this level of Countertop Ice Maker
* Drain plug at the bottom of the unit allows for easy draining for dry storage
* Quiet operation, and you only really hear it when the latest ice drops to the basket
* Touch-control selections for Ice Size, Power, and Wi-Fi activation.
* Long power cord allows for varied position on countertops or other locations

NEUTRAL:
* The system has WI-FI capability (2.4G only and will not work with a 5G internet connection). It is used with the Smart Life app on iPhones (Don't know about Android), and it DOES work, but it seems like more of a sales feature than is likely to be actually used regularly by most owners. In my case, I can't really think of a situation where the app would be a significant benefit. Nonetheless, it is available if you have some specific use for it.
* It is a larger unit than many other countertop Ice Makers and takes a bit more room, all the while making the same amount of ice as others, and the same length of time for production. Ice bin size is equivalent to other makers.

MINOR ANNOYANCES:
* When the "Add Water" light comes on, you must recycle the power switch after filling the water reservoir in order to reactivate the system and recognize water has been added. Other machines do not require a power reset and automatically sense the water level filled and resume production.
* Ice made by this system tends to bunch towards the back of the ice basket which can cause the machine to think the basket is full and suspend ice-making until it doesn't sense ice at the top of the basket. This reduces the amount to ice made at one time because the basket still has room for one or two more cycles of ice production. A simple shake of the basket or moving a little ice forward into the bucket allows the machine to resume.

This is now the third countertop ice maker that we own. We have one in our RV; another on our basement bar, and one more for my wife's office staff for their cold drinks. They are great to have when you either don't have a freezer ice-maker, or need ice away from your kitchen. We use ours on ou outdoor patio as well in the summer for quick access without having to go in the house for ice.

All in all, I rate this Ice Maker at a relatively high FOUR STARS, and not five due to the annoyances mentioned and the size of the unit that does not produce more ice than our other smaller machines. I can still honestly recommend it, as it makes good ice, quickly, and the self-cleaning feature is a real unique bonus. Bon Appetit!

Save your money and buy something better. When I took it back out could hear something rattling around in. Better off just putting a toggle switch on fridge with a thermometer and manually cycling it!

I run a guitar repair shop and it is very important to monitor and maintain a consistent humidity between 45% and 50%. That being said, I need to keep hygrometers that will maintain consistent readings. Please refer to my photos for what I found:

The Indoor Digital Hygrometer Thermometer being reviewed here is the black horizontal one. The black/silver one an AcuRite I purchased at the same time (reviewed elsewhere). The white digital hygrometer is an AcuRite I purchased over a year ago from Home Depot, which has been fairly consistent and reliable. Each of these can sit out in my shop, in a guitar case, or in my storage room.

I let all 3 hygrometers sit together in a corner of my shop about 5 feet away from my dehumidifier for about a week. As you can see, the Indoor Digital Hygrometer Thermometer regularly tracked higher than the 2 AcuRites, often by as much as 5 percentage points, but not consistently. It did not change as readily if moved into a dry part of the building or outside. It appears that 50% is a "sweet spot" and that it tends to lose accuracy as it is exposed to humidity levels higher or lower from there.

I give this 2/5 stars because I intend to keep this and use it (most likely) in a storage room where I do not expect many changes in humidity and where I will keep less wood that will be susceptible to humidity. I will have to keep in mind that it will (on average) measure about 5 percentage points high. I can't say I'd recommend this product since it appears to be unreliable both in accuracy and precision. This appears to be a case of "you get what you pay for."

The original fresh food fan in my GE Adora stopped working. The refrigerator compartment would only cool to 60F. I ordered the fan from Amazon and replaced it in less than an hour. Replacement is plug and play. GE notes that this part, WR60X29099 GE Refrigerator fresh food fan motor, is a replacement part for the original fresh food compartment cooling fan. In the industry, this is called an engineering change. I noticed that the new fan has seven blades, the original has three blades. I can attest that the new fan cools the food compartment lower that when the unit was new. When it was new, the lowest the food compartment would go was 40F. Usually, it varied from 43F to 45F. Now with the new fan, the temp is constantly at 35F. I had to raise the set temp to get it up to 39F – optimal by my experience. Totally satisfied with the part, and happy to have improved my refrigerator's performance, and avoided an expensive repair bill.

Now I will say, when I first opened it, I thought I had received the wrong size. It LOOKED small! I eyed it and thought "this must be a 12 oz"... but I didn't want to be the jerk who sends it back for being the wrong size when it's indeed what I ordered. So I filled it up with water, and then poured it into a measuring cup, and sure enough, it was TWENTY FLUID OUNCES!

So this leads me to believe that other customers have indeed received the correct size and just didn't realize it's smaller than it sounds.

With that being said, if you want to make two lattes at once with this (about 8-10 oz of milk each), you might be able to if you only use 16 oz of milk, but this does not allow much room for the frother wand and froth expansion. I would err on the side of caution and go with the 30 oz size. However this is great if you only want to make single serving drinks! Or cappuccinos since those require less milk.

It's not a serious problem if you first center the flat filter as it comes out of the package and carefully bend up the edges around the retainer plate,  It will hold that shape, and thereafter, inserting the retainer plate into the now-formed screen will center it correctly.  Once the plunger is securely screwed into the bottom plate, the screen will stay centered and coffee grounds will not seem around the oversized hole.  The edge of the screen is trimmed more neatly than the original screen that came with the coffee press, but it still could be done better.  The very edge of the screen should have a double fold so the sharp wires are hidden inside a hem, rather than protruding inside the screen where they can poke the user during cleaning. I ordered this motor since after 20+ years the old one died. I researched the procedure on YouTube for replacement. Very easy! BUT—I would recommend you take the blower fan off your old motor prior to placing your order. The fan is a reverse thread and uses a 7/8 socket to remove. After 20+ years of use, the fan blower was on so tight it wouldn’t budge! I have excellent mechanical skills and have years and years of experience doing this kind of stuff. There was just no removing that fan without destroying it! So back to Amazon I went to order the blower box assembly. Had to wait the shipping time once again. Another weekend without a dryer. So be sure that you will be able to liberate your fan blower assembly from your old motor. If not, you’ll need to order the blower assembly too. The only tip I have is to note the depth of the fan blades on the current fan before you remove it. I did not, and I attached the fan blade too far down the stem of the new fan, and I don't think it was circulating air properly as a result. This simple little coffee brewer will make the best cup of coffee against all other brewers.
It is portable, cheap, and cleans up in 10 seconds - no filters to dump into the landfill.
Here's how you do it:
1) Take 1.5 cups of filtered water and bring to 190F
2) While that's happening measure out 15g of coffee beans and then coarsely grind
3) place brew buddy on a mug and pour 15g of ground coffee in, set on a scale and zero out
4) Pour 50g of 190F water and let sit 30 seconds
5) Add 50g more of hot water, then a final 50g for a total of 150g
6) Remove brew buddy and discard grounds in the trash and rinse with fresh water
7) Add remaining hot water to the mug

That's how you make the best cup of coffee on this planet.
You are doing what folks in the olive oil world have known for hundreds of years - the 1st 1/3 of the pressing is prime - the rest is trash

Everyone in the house loves the size and style of this ice maker as well as the ice it makes. The ice keeps drink perfectly cold for a long time and the enjoyable crunch keeps you going back for more. The ice maker starts out with 2 liters of water and makes a full bin of ice. As you need it, it continues to monitor how full the bin is and makes more when necessary. What's not used, slowly melts back into the reservoir and remade into ice. I thought that the unused ice would melt into a big block if I don't use it but in reality, it stays in the perfect little shape until I need it. The ice bin is easy to access and pouring more water in is easy as well. I didn't get the extra water tank and might later but so far, it's easy to keep up with from a pitcher. When the compressor comes on, you know your ice is being made. It's not too loud though, even with it in the open kitchen. All-in-all, I am very happy with this purchase and highly recommend it.

This is nothing more than a regular bell pitcher that has had a large dimple formed in the base. Be aware that, in my opinion, you are paying for the 'design' rather than for the material quality of the pitcher.

Performance: This pitcher did not do any better at helping to create foam than I can do myself in a regular mug (and I am no expert!) Perhaps if the wand on your machine is long enough and can be positioned vertically into the pitcher, dead center above the dimple, you may get great results (as per some of the other reviewers), but remember that you are still paying a lot for what is basically a pitcher with a dimple in the bottom.

Quality of manufacture: The label on the box says "Canada", on opening, the tag attached to the handle says "Designed in Canada. Made in China." It states that material is 18/8 stainless steel, and there is a 1 year warranty. The handle is spot welded at a total of 6 points. The finish is 'brushed' on the outside and unfinished on the inside (don't expect a smooth mirror finish!) Tooling marks (scratches) going across the brushed finish are clearly visibly around the spout and elsewhere.

Conclusion: Three stars, it's not particularly finished well and it doesn't do (for me) any better of a job at helping to froth the milk.

My wife's old Maytag Gas Dryer, which she will not part with, stopped heating. I looked through the small "peep" hole on the front and saw that the ignitor was glowing hot as it should be I didn't see any flame. So, reading online I saw where the fact that the ignitor was glowing meant that all control circuits were working and the most likely culprit were the gas valve coils.
I got online and ordered them, they arrived the next morning, swapped them out and we are drying once again. It took more time to get the front of the dryer off to obtain access than it did to install them. Make sure you pull the power plug and turn off the gas when making this repair.

After 4 months of trying hard to really like this machine, I'm giving up. It does do what it says it will, but not as fast or as well as it implies. I think that in certain use environments, it might be a satisfactory item, but the use I tried to put it to, that is, a primary ice maker for my second home, it just doesn't cut it. Add the next batch, same result---a bigger block of ice. I guess I'm asking a lot of a relatively inexpensive machine, but I'm simply not going to keep trying to be happy with it. Hopefully I can find someone to give it to who will have a use for it that is more compatible with its true functionality.

Pros: Easy to install, communicates with rest of Yolink integrated system, can provide voice warnings when used with a speaker hub.

I purchased two thermometers that I wanted to integrate with the rest of my Yolink water leak detection and shutoff system along with my speaker hub. Installation was a breeze, scan the qr code, push the "set" button, and you are in. Because I have a Yolink speaker hub I was able to program the thermometers to issue a voice warning when the refrigerator temperature went above 42 deg. F (the high and low temperature limits are programmable). Programming is very easy and intuitive. Without a speaker hub, users will receive an email and notification and, optionally, a text message. Customer service is excellent. Batteries are supposed to last two years or more and the Yolink app shows the battery status of all components. When compared to a calibrated digital thermometer, the Yolink thermometers were well within one degree of accuracy. As a bonus, the thermometer keep a history of temperature and humidity. These thermometers are designed for indoor use. Very pleased with this product.

After using this for about a year, it's overall just ok. Here are the quick points:

Pros:
- compact for the capacity
- customer service

Cons:
- first unit I got was defective
- provided hose that came with both units leaked
- cleans ok, not great
- doesn't seem like it will last long

I bought this unit because my specific space necessitate quite small dimensions... with these dimensions, you probably can't have a bigger capacity.
When I received the first unit, one of the rotating pieces on the bottom of the unit was clanging against another metal piece. After doing some troubleshooting with support (including me doing some manual work, by their direction), they concluded I couldn't fix it myself. So they sent a new unit to me at no charge. I was very impressed with their support.
Both hoses I got leaked... not just from the threading, but in the connection between the metal and hose... so the hoses are basically useless. Getting a high quality metal replacement from Home Depot is easy and cheap enough, but it's just extra cost and effort that shouldn't have been necessary.

The cleaning power is just ok. The spinning power is pretty good, but maybe the unit needs an agitator or something. I varied the amount of detergent to see if this was it... but either it's too much and makes the clothes dry super stiff and starchy, or it's too little and the clothes still smell coming out. I've had a magic chef that had better results, but the dimensions would not have worked for my current place (the magic chef is also bigger, so that could be why, but price was about the same).

Now after about a year, this unit is starting to make sounds that it did not make before... parts are probably knocking against each other due to plastic deformation or joints loosening over time. It's nothing nearly as bad as the unit I replaced, but it doesn't give me confidence. I wash about once per week. I wanted to get this so that I could do small loads I wouldn't want to pay for a full cycle on my building's laundry machines, and hopefully save money in the long run... however, due to the smaller capacity, and because I don't think this will last a very long time, I don't know that it'll last long enough to make up the cost (not to mention that it's worse cleaning).

I ordered this range hood to replace an older model that had a partially glass hood. Take my word for it... the glass looks great in the display room, but quickly becomes a huge maintenance pain when it's actually used. All of the expose portions of this hood are stainless steel and are much easier to maintain.

I think I was lucky because the new range hood had a very similar installation to the old one it replaced, so installation was truly a breeze. Also, the ductwork to exhaust the hood through the ceiling was already in place. My total installation time was about 20 minutes, which is far less that most would experience. Depending upon the degree of DIY skill and the changes required to support the installation, I could see this taking most handy individuals 1 - 2 hours. The core piece of the installation is a single hanger that's security mounted to the wall, and upon which the body of the range hood rests. The other pieces of the installation are the supports for the chimney extension. Again, my installation exhausts through the ceiling and attic, but it's also possible to exhaust the unit through a wall, or to separately purchase filters that can be used to recirculate the exhaust back into the room.

The filters fit easily within the base of the range hood, and are removable and can be cleaned in the dishwasher. I recommend cleaning them in the dishwasher... it's the best way to extract all of the grease that the filters trap. Unlike many similar range hoods (including my old one,) the two downlights are located in the front of the hood rather than the back. This means the two lights product substantially more useful light when using the cooktop. The light is in the 3000K range, which bothers me as I prefer all workspace lighting to be 5000K or greater, but the built-in LED's are bright and provide an excellent level of light for my cooktop. I'm not sure if the LED lights can be replaced with over-the-counter products, but since they are LED's, I suspect they will have a long life.

The lights are turned on and off using a touch-button on the front of the hood, as you can see if the first picture I've enclosed. These buttons only require a minimal tap to activate. Dimming is not an option. The hood has 3 speeds, and I find that the lowest speed is effective for most of my needs. The lowest speed is also very quiet, which is rare among range hoods. The buttons are backlight by a blue LED so you can see at a glance what is on or off. Another button to the right of the light switch is a timer... while the exhaust is running, you can tap this button and walk away knowing that the fan will shut itself off after 5 minutes.

When using the range hood with the external exhaust, it's crucial that the exhaust extend outdoors. This hood requires 6" ductwork, while most common range hoods use 4". The hood has built-in dampers which close the vent when the fan is not in use to prevent backdraft from outside. Be aware that the length of the exhaust pipe, and the number of bends in the pipe will impact the effectiveness of the range hood. Obviously, the shorter and straighter the run of the vent, the most effective the exhaust. Many homeowners do not take this into consideration, but it can have a significant impact on performance and will underscore why some have better experiences than others.

My experience with this exhaust is fantastic... I couldn't be more please with its look and performance. If I had to change one thing, it would be to change the LED lighting to 6,000K so that the color of the light matches everything else in my home. I don't hesitate to recommend this product, but I do recommend that you either be knowledgeable regarding electricity and ductwork, or have a professional perform the installation.

We own an older version of this burner and it does a good job of providing a reliable source of heat for your pots and pans, but the knobs are poorly made.  After eight years of use, all four of the knobs have cracks in the bottom cylinders which fit over the metal controls in the main part of the heating unit; the knobs began failing two years ago.  If you do a search for replacement Bosch knobs you will find that other folks have had similar problems.  We filed a complaint with Bosch and their management team reviewed the photos that we sent and determined that because it had been eight years since we purchased the stove top that they would not be willing to provide replacement knobs. They best that they could do would be to offer us a 20% discount on the cost of the knobs which the sell for approximately $25 each. Given that the total cost of replacement would still be about $100 for four knobs, we declined their "generous" offer. I find it ridiculous that a major appliance corporation seems to feel that their knobs should not last more than six to eight years from regular use in normal conditions.  We take good care of our appliances and vehicles. I have a Kenmore washer/dryer set that last almost 20 years without any major repairs being needed. Sears makes some great knobs!  And I owned my last automobile for 13 years without it needing major repairs; Honda Pilot's are awesome.  So, if you don't mind taking the risk that the knobs on your appliance will fail after six to eight years of use, then this is the stove top for you. Otherwise, I would consider another brand.

This works fine on my LG washer model WM2101HW. The local parts place wanted $100 to replace both the broken plastic spline and the magnetic casing even though these clearly are separate parts and there was nothing wrong with the casing. The original part came with a plastic spline that wore out preventing the drum from rotating. This part is mostly plastic but has a metal spline built into it. In the end, this may just shift the failure point to a different location but overall, it looks much better designed. Ideally, it would be better if this were made completely of metal.

Installation is fairly simple but getting enough torque on the bolt that holds the part on can be tricky since the drum rotates as you tighten it down. It took two people with one holding the drum in place and the other tightening the bolt. I don't know an exact torque value but several years ago, the factory tightened drum loosened itself which likely did a great deal of the damage to the original spline. I made sure this was pretty tight.

My only issue with this order was with delivery. It took a long time to ship and even longer after my credit card had to be cancelled a few days after my original order. It really shouldn't take 11 days to get a part this small - especially one that is critical for the functioning of a major appliance.

After years of having to go to laundromat I am finally able to have a washer and dryer but with no hookups I needed something portable. To me this is like any full size dryer except smaller. It heats just fine. The drum is a really good size. I have of family of four. My teen sons are 6'3 and 5'11 and I've dried loads of their clothes with 5 sometimes 6 pairs of jeans and 4 shirts and some underwear in one load a lil over an hr. If u use the eco setting the dryer air will not be hot. I always put the heat setting on the red even if i only need it for 15 mins because it won't be as hot cuz the lower numbers are less heat. When I do that I just set a timer for however long I need. The dryer sits in my kitchen and throws out about as much heat as if I'm using the oven. I vent it into one of those portable vent buckets with water in it and put the bucket in one of those bags u use to wash your delicates to catch any lint coming out of bucket. I just love my lil dryer.
